Tested through CL's Quality Certification Program prior to, or after initial posting of this Product Review.
NA - Not applicable

* Product identical in formulation and manufacture to a product that has passed testing but sold under a different brand. For more information see CL's Multi-Label Testing Program.

1 Products that contain root/rhizome powder from E. purpurea and E. angustifolia, the expected to contain a minimum of 0.5% TPCs of the claimed amount of powder. For products containing aerial parts from E. purpurea, the expected to contain a minimum of 0.5% TPCs of the claimed amount of powder. All extracts must provide a minimum of 4% TPCs.
2 Only products that contain whole herb(s) and/or more than 250 mg of minerals daily were tested for lead, cadmium and arsenic.
3 Only tested on products that contained Echinacea powder (not extract).
4 Not tested but claimed on label.
5 Label states "Adults: 20-25 drops (Children from age 2: 1 drop per year of age) in a small amount of water 3 times a day.
6 Since the product claims to be derived from fresh extracts, a minimum of 1% TPCs for herb extract (from Commission E and USP), and a minimum 0.6% TPCs for root extract (from Commission E) were applied.
7 Label states "Adults take 2 capsules every 2 hours at onset (2 to 4 days) or as directed by your health care provider."
8 Label states "3 times daily. Children under age 6: 15 drops, age 6-10: 30 drops, age 11-adult: 60 drops."
9 Since no ingredient weight is list, unable to determine an expected minimum.
10 Product was "Not Approved" because an ingredient weight is not listed — a violation of FDA labeling rules. The product only lists a volume of liquid (2 mL) of "Proprietary Extract Blend." Additionally, this volume is the same as that listed as the full size of a serving, suggesting that it includes substances other than the extract blend, such as vegetable glycerin and water which are listed as "Other ingredients."  
11 Based on price listed on puritan.com. Price is for a "Buy 1 Get 1 Free" deal.
12 Based on price listed on vitaminworld.com.
13 Label states "Infants 6 months to 1 yr: 5 drops, 4x daily. Children 1 - 12 yrs: 10 drops, 4x daily".
14 Label states "As a dietary supplement, parents may give each child, four years old or older, up to two (2) gummy bears per day." 15 Based on a 20 drop serving.

Unless otherwise noted, information about the products listed above is based on the samples purchased by ConsumerLab.com (CL) for this Product Review. Manufacturers may change ingredients and label information at any time, so be sure to check labels carefully when evaluating the products you use or buy. If a product's ingredients differ from what is listed above, it may not necessarily be of the same quality as what was tested.

The information contained in this report is based on the compilation and review of information from product labeling and analytic testing. CL applies what it believes to be the most appropriate testing methods and standards. The information in this report does not reflect the opinion or recommendation of CL, its officers or employees. CL cannot assure the accuracy of information.
